flows.name: Play a complete Snake game

steps.action: Clicks 'New Game' button
Resets game state: snake at center (3 segments), food spawned randomly, score 0, generation 0, speed 5 FPS
Starts game tick scheduler, begins RAF loop, button label changes to 'Pause'
Presses arrow keys to move snake
Buffers input, executes move on next tick, updates grid state
Each tick: check food collision, grow snake, increment speed if food eaten, check wall/self collision
Snake eats food, score increments
Snake grows, new food spawns, speed increases by 1 FPS
Snake hits wall or itself
Game ends, tick scheduler stops, records final game to replay list, displays score and 'Game Over'

postconditions: Final game recorded in replay list
User can select replay or start new game

error_paths.trigger: User presses reverse direction (e.g., right arrow then left arrow rapidly)
error_paths.system_response: Input buffering prevents snake from reversing; only most recent valid direction processed

error_paths.trigger: Food spawns on exact cell where snake head moves
error_paths.system_response: Head reaches food on that tick; snake grows, new food spawns at different location

flows.keyboard_path: Tab to game canvas → Arrow keys for movement → Tab to Pause button → Enter to pause

flows.name: Pause and resume active game
flows.trigger: User clicks 'Pause' button during active game
preconditions: Pause button is visible and enabled

steps.action: Clicks 'Pause' button
Stops RAF loop, halts game tick execution, button label changes to 'Resume'
Grid remains in current state (snake, food, score visible)
Clicks 'Resume' button
Resumes RAF loop, game continues from paused state

error_paths.trigger: User clicks Pause on already paused game
error_paths.system_response: Pause button is disabled when game is paused; no effect

flows.name: Step through one generation at a time
flows.trigger: User clicks 'Step' button while game is paused

steps.action: Clicks 'Step' button
Executes one game tick without starting RAF loop
Updates snake position, checks collisions, updates score if food eaten
Renders updated grid, generation counter increments
Clicks Step again or clicks Play to resume

error_paths.system_response: Step button is disabled; no effect

flows.name: Select and watch a replay at chosen speed
preconditions: At least one completed game saved

steps.action: Clicks 'View Replays' button
Displays list of completed games with score, timestamp, duration
Clicks a replay from the list
Loads replay states, selects speed (default 1×), starts playback animation
Renders ghost snake moving through recorded game sequence
Clicks speed control to change multiplier (0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×)
Speed multiplier updated, replay continues at new speed
Replay finishes, displays 'Replay Complete'
Clicks 'Back' button

error_paths.system_response: Replay list is empty; message 'No replays available' displayed

flows.keyboard_path: Tab to View Replays → Enter → Tab through replay list → Enter to select → Tab to speed controls → Arrow keys to adjust

flows.name: Play live game while watching ghost replay simultaneously
flows.trigger: User clicks 'New Game' while a replay is running

steps.action: New game is running (see FLOW-1)
Ghost snake continues playing replay independently
Canvas renders both live snake (solid color) and ghost snake (faded color) simultaneously
Live game and ghost replay tick independently; no collision logic between them
Lives game ends on collision while ghost replay continues
Records completed live game; ghost replay continues until finished
Clicks 'Stop Replay' to end ghost visualization
Ghost snake disappears, live game state remains on canvas

postconditions: User can start new game or view menu

error_paths.trigger: Ghost snake occupies same cell as live snake
error_paths.system_response: No collision logic applied; both render normally in separate visual layers

flows.keyboard_path: Same as FLOW-1 for live game + Tab to Stop Replay button for ghost

flows.name: Stop active game and return to menu
flows.trigger: User clicks 'Stop' button during active game or replay

steps.action: Clicks 'Stop' button
Halts game tick or replay playback immediately
Clears canvas or returns to neutral state
Returns to main menu with buttons: 'New Game', 'View Replays'
Can now start a new game or select a replay

postconditions: Last game NOT automatically recorded if stopped mid-play

error_paths.trigger: User clicks Stop during paused game
error_paths.system_response: Game ends and is NOT recorded (only completed games are recorded)

flows.name: Delete a replay
flows.trigger: User clicks delete icon on replay in the replay list

steps.action: Clicks delete/trash icon next to a replay
Shows confirmation dialog 'Delete this replay?'
Clicks 'Confirm Delete'
Removes replay from list and localStorage
Updates replay list display

postconditions: User can verify in replay list

error_paths.system_response: Replay is NOT deleted, dialog closes

flows.keyboard_path: Tab to replay in list → Tab to delete button → Enter → Tab to Confirm → Enter

flows.name: Handle invalid rapid key presses (reverse direction prevention)
flows.trigger: User presses two opposite directions (e.g., Right then Left) in rapid succession

steps.action: Presses Right arrow, then immediately presses Left arrow before next tick
Input handler buffers both directions in queue
On next tick, input handler validates: Left would cause reverse (snake moving Right)
Invalid direction ignored; only the valid queued direction (or last direction) executed
Snake does NOT move into itself; game continues normally

postconditions: Snake continues moving in safe direction

error_paths.trigger: User performs same key sequence multiple times
error_paths.system_response: Consistently prevents reverse direction on every iteration

states.name: Menu
states.description: Initial state and return state. Main menu with game options visible.
states.ui_condition: Buttons visible: 'New Game', 'View Replays'; Canvas empty or neutral; No game running

states.name: Game-Playing
states.description: Active game in progress. Snake moving, score updating, ticks occurring.
states.ui_condition: Canvas shows live snake, food, score counter active; Pause button visible; Step disabled

states.name: Game-Paused
states.description: Game paused, not advancing. Player can step or resume.
states.ui_condition: Canvas frozen on current state; Resume button visible; Step button enabled

states.name: Game-Over
states.description: Game ended on collision. Final score displayed. Game ready to be viewed as replay.
states.ui_condition: Canvas shows final snake position; 'Game Over' message visible; Score displayed; 'New Game' and 'View Replays' available

states.name: Replay-List
states.description: Browsing list of saved replays. Can select to play or delete.
states.ui_condition: List shows all recorded games with scores, timestamps; Scrollable; Delete icons visible

states.name: Replay-Playing
states.description: Watching a recorded game replay. Ghost snake animating through recorded states.
states.ui_condition: Canvas shows ghost snake (faded); Speed controls visible; Pause, Resume, Stop buttons available

states.name: Live-Plus-Replay
states.description: Simultaneous live game and ghost replay. Both visible on canvas, ticking independently.
states.ui_condition: Canvas shows live snake (solid) and ghost snake (faded) simultaneously; Both game and replay controls available

accessibility_requirements: All interactive elements (buttons, controls) reachable by Tab key
Canvas has aria-label='Snake game grid' for screen readers
Score counter has aria-live='polite' to announce updates to screen readers
Game-over message and status changes announced via aria-live regions
Replay list is accessible with keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter to select)
Speed controls have clear labels (0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×) with keyboard shortcuts (1, 2, 3, 4 keys)
Error messages associated to relevant UI elements via aria-describedby
Keyboard-only players do not lose any functionality — no mouse-only actions
ambiguities: Speed increase formula assumed linear (1 FPS per food); actual speed cap not specified in requirements
Ghost snake visual distinction (color/opacity) not specified; left to implementation
Replay list sorting order not specified; assumed chronological (newest first)
localStorage quota exceeded behavior not specified; assumed warn user and offer delete options
Keyboard shortcut keys for speed control (1, 2, 3, 4) assumed but not requested in requirements
